dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S5015 IS: Broadband Mapping Accurate Providers Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Thune (for himself, Mr. Fetterman, and Mrs. Fischer) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To require the Federal Communications Commission to review and evaluate the processes for updating maps that depict the extent of the availability of broadband internet access service in the United States, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Broadband Mapping Accurate Providers Act of 2026 or the Broadband MAP Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: The term broadband internet access service has the meaning given the term in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.
The term broadband maps means the maps created under section 802(c)(1) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 642(c)(1)).
The term broadband serviceable location means an area in the United States where a provider of broadband internet access service provides, or could provide, broadband internet access service.
The term challenge process means the challenge process established pursuant to section 802(b)(5) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 642(b)(5)).
The term Commission means the Federal Communications Commission.

subsection.text: Not later than 120 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Commission shall initiate a rulemaking to review and evaluate the processes for updating the broadband maps, including the challenge process.
In the rulemaking initiated under subsection (b), the Commission shall consider—

paragraph.text: the relative equities and burdens of any proposed changes to the broadband maps;
the impact of any proposed changes with respect to the use of the broadband maps when making funding decisions;
the impact of any changes proposed in that rulemaking on providers of broadband internet access service; and
ongoing Commission efforts relating to broadband mapping, the restoration of broadband serviceable locations, and the challenge process.
